overview.tagline: Where the Palisades Meet Corporate America
overview.description: Englewood Cliffs is a small, affluent borough perched atop the Palisades cliffs overlooking the Hudson River. Despite its compact size, it is home to major corporate headquarters including LG Electronics North America and formerly Unilever, making it a significant corporate campus destination. The borough combines exclusive residential neighborhoods, dramatic cliff-top vistas, and a prestigious address just minutes from Manhattan via the George Washington Bridge.
highlights: Perched atop the dramatic Palisades cliffs with Hudson River panoramas
Home to LG Electronics North America headquarters and other major corporations
Exclusive residential community with high property values
Direct access to Palisades Interstate Park and scenic overlooks
Minutes from the George Washington Bridge and Manhattan

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Englewood Cliffs features large residential lots with significant corporate ratables helping offset residential tax burden.
realEstate.recentTrends: Englewood Cliffs remains one of the most expensive real estate markets in Bergen County. The presence of major corporate ratables keeps residential tax rates relatively low. New luxury construction has increased, with homes often exceeding $2 million. The market attracts wealthy professionals and international buyers.
neighborhoodNotes: The Palisade Avenue corridor features stately homes on large lots with mature landscaping
Properties along the cliff edge command premium prices for Hudson River and Manhattan views
The corporate campus zone along Sylvan Avenue includes LG headquarters and other office buildings
The northern section near the Alpine border has some of the most secluded and expensive properties

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by location — check the borough website
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally in biodegradable bags

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges included in utility or tax bills
Water quality reports published annually
Report water emergencies to the DPW or Veolia

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Englewood Cliffs Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Tree removal permits may be required for protected specimens
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Englewood Cliffs is primarily a residential and corporate campus community with limited municipal parking needs.
details: Street parking subject to posted regulations
No overnight parking on borough streets
Corporate campuses have private parking facilities
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth sports, camps, and community activities.
details: Youth sports leagues including soccer, basketball, and baseball
Summer recreation camp for children
Community events and seasonal celebrations
Borough pool facility (seasonal)

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Englewood Cliffs Public Library provides resources and programs for residents.
details: Located at 110 Charlotte Place
Children's and adult programming
Computer access and reference services
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Englewood Cliffs enforces strict noise restrictions to protect its quiet residential character.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:30 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment (leaf blowers, mowers) restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise complaints handled by the police department

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to the borough's high standards.
keyRules: Lawns and landscaping must be professionally maintained
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of debris, junk, or abandoned vehicles
Trees and hedges must not obstruct sidewalks or sight lines
Swimming pools must be fenced and meet safety codes

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled at all times within the borough.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for dogs and cats
Barking dog complaints may result in fines

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Englewood Cliffs enforces parking rules to maintain the residential and corporate character of the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets
No parking on lawns or unpaved areas
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
Construction vehicle parking requires a permit

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Englewood Cliffs has limited rental housing; regulations ensure standards are maintained.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental properties with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
Short-term rentals may be subject to zoning restrictions
rentals.source: Englewood Cliffs Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Englewood Cliffs has a tree preservation ordinance protecting mature trees on private and public property.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees above a specified caliper on residential property
Replacement planting may be required for trees removed
Protected species along the Palisades cliffs have additional protections
Fines for unauthorized tree removal can be significant

publicSchools.name: Upper School (Englewood Cliffs Upper School)
publicSchools.notes: Small school with excellent academic programs; students attend Dwight Morrow High School in Englewood for grades 9-12

publicSchools.name: North Cliff School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school with small class sizes and strong parent involvement

privateSchools.name: Elisabeth Morrow School (nearby Englewood)
privateSchools.notes: Prestigious independent school in neighboring Englewood serving many Englewood Cliffs families

privateSchools.name: Dwight-Englewood School (nearby Englewood)
privateSchools.notes: Top independent school in neighboring Englewood, popular with Englewood Cliffs residents

whyMoveHere: Exclusive, affluent community with large lots and high-end homes
Low residential property tax rate thanks to significant corporate ratables
Dramatic Palisades cliff setting with Hudson River and Manhattan views
Excellent small public schools with high student-teacher ratios
Minutes from the George Washington Bridge for quick Manhattan access

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: Car via George Washington Bridge / NJ Transit Bus
commuteToNYC.duration: 15-35 minutes by car; 25-45 minutes by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Most residents commute by car via Route 9W to the George Washington Bridge. NJ Transit bus routes are available from Palisade Avenue. The nearby Edgewater ferry is another option for commuters heading to Midtown Manhattan.

costOfLiving.summary: Englewood Cliffs has a very high cost of living driven by premium home prices, though property tax rates are moderate due to corporate ratables.

pros: Lower effective tax rate due to corporate ratables (LG, etc.)
Exclusive community with beautiful homes and large lots
Stunning Palisades cliff-top location with river views
Very close to Manhattan via GWB
cons: Very high home prices — median over $1 million
Limited walkability — car-dependent for most errands
No downtown commercial district or Main Street
Students attend Englewood high school for grades 9-12
Limited rental and starter home inventory

restaurants.name: Aqua Blu
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian restaurant offering fresh seafood, pasta, and an extensive wine list in an elegant setting.

restaurants.name: Bâton Rouge
restaurants.description: Cajun and Creole-inspired restaurant serving Louisiana-style cuisine including gumbo, jambalaya, and blackened fish.

restaurants.name: White Castle (Original Site)
restaurants.description: The fast-food chain's corporate test kitchen location near the former headquarters, a piece of American fast-food history.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Englewood Cliffs Section
parks.description: Dramatic cliff-top parkland running along the Hudson River with hiking trails, scenic overlooks, and the Englewood Boat Basin at the river's edge.
parks.address: Palisades Interstate Park, Englewood Cliffs, NJ 07632

parks.name: Englewood Cliffs Recreation Area
parks.description: Borough recreation complex with ball fields, basketball courts, a playground, and a community pool facility.

parks.name: Joseph A. Gianfortone Memorial Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with a playground, picnic areas, and green space for community gatherings.

events.name: Englewood Cliffs Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with games, rides, food vendors, and live entertainment for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony with holiday music, refreshments, and community festivities.

attractions.name: Palisades Scenic Overlooks
attractions.description: Multiple scenic viewpoints along the Palisades cliffs offering spectacular views of the Hudson River, GWB, and Manhattan skyline.

attractions.name: LG Electronics North America Headquarters
attractions.description: The striking modern glass headquarters of LG Electronics, a major architectural landmark visible from the Palisades.

attractions.name: Englewood Boat Basin
attractions.description: A riverside marina and recreation area at the base of the Palisades cliffs, accessible from the park with boat launch facilities and picnic areas.
attractions.address: Palisades Interstate Park, Englewood Cliffs, NJ

shoppingAreas: Sylvan Avenue — Corporate corridor with limited retail and dining options
Nearby Englewood downtown — Boutiques, restaurants, and services on Palisade Avenue (1 mile south)
Nearby Edgewater — River Road retail corridor with Mitsuwa Marketplace and major stores (2 miles south)

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Englewood Cliffs Borough Hall, 557 Palisade Avenue, Englewood Cliffs, NJ 07632
